user_query:
Create an AI Education Daily report from the provided candidate articles. Read input/articles.json (two arrays: wechat and web), input/report_config.json (contains report_date and 3–4 focus keywords), and input/blacklist.txt (one keyword per line). Using only these inputs (no external browsing), do the following:

1) Filtering and window:
- Exclude any article published more than 7 days before report_date.
- Exclude any article whose title or summary matches any blacklist term (case-insensitive, substring match).

2) Scoring (four dimensions, max 10, include if total >= 4.5):
- Recency (max 3): today=2, yesterday=1.5, day_before=1, 3–7 days ago=1.
- Practicality (max 3): immediate=2 if title/summary contains any of ["guide", "how to", "checklist", "tool", "rct", "experiment"]; reference=1 if it contains any of ["case", "story", "experience", "practice", "classroom", "school"]; else 0.
- Authority (max 1): 1 if source_category is "official" or "media"; 0.5 otherwise.
- Audience fit (max 3): 2 if both parent-signals and teacher-signals appear; 1.5 if either appears; 0.5 otherwise. Parent-signals: ["parent", "kid", "child", "mom", "dad", "family", "homework", "grades"]. Teacher-signals: ["teacher", "classroom", "school", "curriculum", "educator"].
- If source_category is "marketing", set practicality to 0 regardless of keywords.

3) Deduplication before inclusion:
- Consider titles duplicates if similarity > 0.6 by token Jaccard (case-insensitive) or if they obviously describe the same event in different wording.
- When duplicates occur, keep the item with higher authority (official > media > others). Remove the others with reason "duplicate".

4) Categorization for the report:
- Parent Cases: items clearly about families/parents.
- Practice Frontline: items about schools/classrooms or concrete implementation.
- Policy Signals: items about government policies, standards, or official plans.
- Quick News: 3 single-line bullets labeled Parent Community, Product Updates, Research Frontiers (use remaining eligible items or synthesize concise summaries consistent with selected items).

5) Outputs to create:
- output/AI_Education_Daily/AI_Education_Daily_2026-04-02.md — A structured Markdown report in English with this exact section order and clear headers:
  a) YAML front matter with: date=2026-04-02, title="AI Education Daily - 2026-04-02", status: draft, keywords: [from input/report_config.json], and a one-sentence summary.
  b) "# AI Education Daily - April 2, 2026" title line, then intro line "For parents and educators following AI in education".
  c) "## 🏷️ Today’s Keywords" — list the keywords.
  d) "## 💡 Actionable Suggestions" — two numbered items for parents and two for educators.
  e) "## 👨‍👩‍👧 Parent Cases" — at least one item with a "###" subheading, a source/time line (Source: X | Time: YYYY-MM-DD), and a 100–200 word body.
  f) "## 🔬 Practice Frontline" — include both a "### School Case" and a "### Home Exploration" entry, each with source/time line and 100–200 word body.
  g) "## 📢 Policy Signals" — at least one item with a "###" subheading, source/time line, and a 100–200 word body.
  h) "## ⚡ Quick News" — three one-line bullets labeled Parent Community, Product Updates, Research Frontiers.
  i) "## ✨ Quote" — one suitable quote with attribution.
- output/selection.json — Array of included articles with fields: {title, source, source_category, type ("wechat"|"web"), date (YYYY-MM-DD), score (number), breakdown: {recency, practicality, authority, audience}, category ("parent"|"practice"|"policy"|"other")}.
- output/rejections.json — Array of excluded articles with fields: {title, source, reason} where reason is one of ["blacklist", "stale", "below_threshold", "duplicate"].

Additional requirements:
- Ensure no selected title includes promotional language like "buy", "coupon", "deal", or "discount".
- Ensure all included items are within 7 days of report_date (2026-04-02).
- Ensure uniqueness of titles in selection.json.
- Write everything in English. Do not call external services. Use only the input files.
